pr.title: Fix code-inventory/self-model reporting zero modules & edges on Windows (path separator)
pr.description:
## Summary

Windows lane-2 found `execution-chain-run` producing `module_count=0`, `import_edge_count=0`, `isolated_module_count=0` **even after** `code-inventory --refresh` populated records. Root cause is a Windows path-separator bug in the inventory scanner — **not** empty inventory (this is exactly the "inventory-category / self-model-consumption mismatch" Plamen suspected).

`CodebaseInventory.scan()` and `extract_import_edges()` computed the repo-relative path with `str(Path.relative_to(repo_root))`, which uses the **OS separator**. On Windows that yields `src\axiom_core\...`, and every downstream check assumes forward slashes:

```python
# _categorize:            "src\\axiom_core\\x.py".startswith("src/")  -> False  => FileCategory.OTHER (not SOURCE/TEST/CLI)
# extract_import_edges:   not rel.startswith("src/")                  -> True   => every file skipped => 0 edges
# _module_name:           "src\\..\\x.py".replace("/", ".")          -> no-op   => malformed id, then dropped by
#                          self_model.build()'s  f.path.startswith("src/")      => 0 modules
```

So on Windows `SelfModelBuilder.build()` returns zero modules/edges → the chain reports zero metrics → (separately) promotion happily accepts it. Same failure family as the Windows artifact-path bug fixed in PR #151.

**Fix:** normalize once, at both scan sites, via a small helper:

```python
@staticmethod
def _rel_posix(fpath: Path, root: Path) -> str:
    return fpath.relative_to(root).as_posix()   # no-op on POSIX; forward slashes on Windows
```

`.as_posix()` is a no-op on Linux, so **CI/Linux behavior is unchanged**; it only corrects Windows.

**Test:** `test_rel_posix_normalizes_windows_separators` uses `PureWindowsPath` to reproduce backslash paths cross-platform (so it would fail against the old `str(...)` on Linux CI too), and asserts `_categorize`/`_module_name` then classify the normalized path correctly.

## What changed / did not change
- **Changed:** path normalization in `codebase_inventory.py` (2 call sites) + 1 regression test + BUG-019 log entry.
- **Not changed:** inventory schema, categories, module-name rules, self-model/chain/promotion logic, and all Linux behavior.

## Tests run
- `tests/test_codebase_inventory.py`, `tests/test_self_model.py`, `tests/test_execution_chain_orchestrator.py` → 65 passed. Ruff clean. (Targeted per testing-tier policy — Python-only change.)

## Validation still pending
- **Windows lane-2 confirmation by Plamen:** on `C:\Dev\Axiom\Code\Axiom-platform`, re-run `code-inventory --refresh` then `execution-chain-run --json-output` and confirm `module_count > 0` / `import_edge_count > 0`. Not claimed until you run it.
- Does not touch Revit; 2024 baseline unaffected.

## Relationship to Finding 2
This fixes the *mechanical* zero-metric cause on Windows. The *semantic* gate (chain `status=PASS` + promotion accepting genuinely-degenerate zero-metric evidence and jumping `very_low` → `very_high`) is **still worthwhile** and is returned separately as a design diff for your approval before coding — it is NOT in this PR.

## Known risks
- Low. On POSIX `as_posix()` == prior `str()` output, so no behavior change off Windows.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev

commits.message:
Fix code-inventory/self-model reporting zero modules on Windows (path separator normalization)
